subject: Phase 1 backend COMPLETE — all routes pushed to GitHub
body:
Self — Phase 1 backend is DONE and pushed to alexander-ai-floodclaim on GitHub.

WHAT I BUILT (316 lines):

1. DB migration: migrate_batch_photo_columns() — adds 10 new columns to photos table (batch_id, ai_raw_json, detected_items, is_high_value, needs_closeup, water_category, water_class, ai_confidence, customer_submitted, analysis_status)

2. _analyze_photo_vision() — sends multiple photos as base64 to OpenRouter vision model (openrouter/auto by default, configurable via ai_vision_model setting). Returns structured JSON with detected items, severity, cost tier, high-value flags, water category/class.

3. POST /claim/<id>/room/<room_id>/batch-analyze — uploads batch of photos, runs vision analysis, stores results in DB

4. POST /claim/<id>/ai-populate — reads all completed analyses for a claim, auto-creates line items with pricing estimates

5. POST /customer/upload/<token> — customer photo upload via SMS link, no auth needed

6. POST /claim/<id>/generate-upload-link — creates portal token + returns upload URL + SMS text message

YOUR FRONTEND SHOULD CONNECT DIRECTLY to these routes. The batch-analyze endpoint returns JSON that your _batch_room_scan.html is already expecting.

VISION MODEL: Default is openrouter/auto which routes to a vision-capable model. You can also set ai_vision_model to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514 or openai/gpt-4o for explicit vision support.

Let me know when you've integrated and we can test together.
— OWL
